level1_segments.summary: The circulator nurse assists the scrub nurse and holding a surgical instrument with brief pauses.
The circulator nurse continues to hold surgical instruments and assist the scrub nurse through the final recorded segments.
The circulator nurse holds surgical instruments for an extended period while intermittently assisting the scrub nurse.
The circulator nurse holds surgical instruments and assists the scrub nurse with brief pauses in between.
The circulator nurse holds surgical instrument before preparing operating table and  patient
The circulator nurse holds surgical instruments and assists the assistant surgeon with intermittent idle periods.
The circulator nurse prepares the patient and then holds surgical instruments with a brief pause in between.

The circulator nurse assists the scrub nurse and robot technician while holding surgical instruments.
The circulator nurse manipulates the operating table.

level2_segments.summary: The circulator nurse provides continuous support by holding instruments and assisting the scrub nurse during the initial phase of the procedure.
The circulator nurse shifts focus to assist the assistant surgeon and perform patient preparation while maintaining instrument support.
The circulator nurse manages imaging equipment, coordinates with the robot technician, and adjusts the operating table to facilitate the final stages of the surgery.

level1_segments.summary: The head surgeon prepares the patient and handles surgical instruments in a series of short, consecutive actions.
The head surgeon performs drilling on the patient, interspersed with brief pauses and touching the patient with instruments.
The head surgeon calibrates the Mako robotic arm and performs cutting actions on the patient.
The head surgeon engages in brief cutting and hammering actions separated by short idle periods and instrument handling.
The head surgeon calibrates the Mako robotic arm and prepares the bone saw before performing the sawing procedure on the patient.
The head surgeon pauses briefly, touches the patient, and then uses a hammer on the surgical site.
After a long pause, the surgeon prepares instruments, applies cement to the patient, performs a brief hammering action, and concludes by suturing the wound.
The head surgeon performs suturing on the patient while holding a surgical instrument.
The head surgeon drills into the patient while holding a drill.

level2_segments.summary: The head surgeon prepares the patient and performs initial drilling on the surgical site.
The surgeon calibrates the robotic arm and executes bone cutting and sawing procedures.
The head surgeon applies cement, hammers the implant, and begins suturing the wound.
The head surgeon performs a final drilling action on the patient.

level1_segments.summary: The circulator nurse holds the instrument and briefly shares it with the scrub nurse before a pause.
The circulator nurse transfers the instrument to the robot technician, who then holds it.
The circulator nurse retrieves the instrument and passes it to the scrub nurse for use.
The instrument is briefly held by the circulator and robot technician before being passed to the scrub nurse.
The circulator nurse holds the instrument during a long idle period.
The robot technician and circulator nurse handle the instrument before it is transferred to the scrub nurse.
The circulator nurse holds the instrument and then shares it with the scrub nurse.
The instrument is passed from the circulator nurse to the robot technician and then to the scrub nurse.
The circulator nurse holds the instrument and briefly shares it with the scrub nurse.
The surgical instrument is repeatedly passed between the circulator and scrub nurses during a series of short interactions and brief pauses.
The circulator nurse holds the surgical instrument for an extended period of over eight minutes.
The instrument is transferred back and forth between the scrub nurse and circulator nurse with brief interruptions.
The robot technician briefly takes possession of the surgical instrument.
The scrub nurse holds the instrument for a long duration with brief exchanges involving the circulator nurse.
The instrument is passed to the head surgeon for use, with brief handoffs involving the circulator and scrub nurses.
The surgical instrument is actively used by the head surgeon with brief transfers to the scrub nurse and robot technician for adjustments or handoffs.
The instrument is passed between the circulator nurse, scrub nurse, and robot technician, indicating a phase of preparation, cleaning, or reconfiguration.

level2_segments.summary: The instrument undergoes a series of initial handoffs and brief exchanges between the circulator, robot technician, and scrub nurses during the early preparation phase.
The instrument experiences extended periods of holding by the circulator and scrub nurses, interspersed with transfers and a brief interaction with the robot technician.
The instrument is actively utilized by the head surgeon for the main surgical procedure, with occasional handoffs to support staff for adjustments.
The instrument enters a final phase of preparation, cleaning, or reconfiguration involving multiple handoffs between the circulator, scrub nurse, and robot technician.

level1_segments.summary: The robot technician performs initial setup and calibration by manipulating the robotic arm, workstation, tracker, and monitor.
The technician prepares surgical instruments and operates the robotic arm and tracker while intermittently checking the workstation and monitor.
The technician holds surgical instrument while assisting circulation nurse and scrub nurse.
The robot technician manipulates the Mako robotic arm for an extended period.
The robot technician performs intermittent manipulations of the MPS workstation separated by short idle gaps.
The robot technician assists the scrub nurse and manipulates the robotic arm, followed by brief pauses and a short arm adjustment.
The robot technician manipulates the MPS workstation and infrared tracker with intervening idle periods.
The robot technician briefly adjusts the robotic arm, pauses, and then assists the circulator nurse while preparing the patient.
The robot technician briefly manipulates the Mako robotic arm after a period of inactivity.
The technician prepares the patient and then holds a surgical instrument with brief pauses in between.
The robot technician interacts with the MPS workstation, separated by short idle gaps.
The technician prepares the patient followed by a long period of inactivity.
The technician assists the head surgeon, manipulates the robotic arm, and briefly touches the workstation amidst short pauses.
The technician prepares the patient and then engages in extended manipulation of the MPS workstation with intermittent pauses.
The technician holds a surgical instrument, manipulates the infrared tracker, and operates the MPS workstation in a continuous sequence.
The robot technician manipulates the MPS workstation and infrared tracker to perform initial system adjustments.
The robot technician briefly interacts with the Mako robotic arm and infrared tracker amidst short pauses.
The robot technician resumes manipulating the MPS workstation with short pauses.
The robot technician transitions from manipulating the Mako robotic arm to touching and manipulating the MPS workstation.
The robot technician intermittently touches and manipulates the MPS workstation with brief pauses in between.
The robot technician manipulates the Mako robotic arm with short pauses interspersed throughout the activity.
The robot technician manipulated the C-arm, paused, and briefly touched the patient.
The robot technician prepared the instrument table and then paused.
The robot technician manipulated the Mako robotic arm and then paused.
The robot technician assisted the scrub nurse and then paused.
The robot technician held a surgical instrument and then paused.
The robot technician manipulated the Mako robotic arm and then paused.
The robot technician manipulated the MPS workstation and then briefly adjusted the Mako robotic arm.

level2_segments.summary: The robot technician performs initial system setup, calibration, and instrument preparation.
The technician engages in a prolonged phase of intermittent robotic arm manipulation, workstation interaction, and patient preparation.
The robot technician focuses on detailed system adjustments and continuous manipulation of the workstation and robotic arm.
The technician performs final procedural tasks including C-arm manipulation, instrument table preparation, and brief robotic adjustments.

level1_segments.summary: The scrub nurse remains idle with a brief moment of holding an instrument before returning to inactivity.
The scrub nurse engages in a continuous sequence of preparing the instrument table and holding surgical instruments, interspersed with short pauses.
The scrub nurse manipulates the Mako robotic arm while holding various surgical instruments, including a bone saw.
The scrub nurse calibrates the Mako robotic arm and intermittently prepares the instrument table.
The scrub nurse manipulates the Mako robotic arm and holds surgical instruments with brief pauses in between.
The scrub nurse prepares the instrument table and assists the assistant surgeon intermittently with short idle gaps.
The scrub nurse alternates between preparing the instrument table and holding surgical instruments with brief pauses.
The scrub nurse cleans and prepares the patient, including handling surgical drapes, with intermittent short breaks.
The scrub nurse prepares the instrument table and assists the head surgeon by holding surgical instruments with brief pauses in between.
The scrub nurse alternates between preparing the instrument table, holding instruments, and assisting with patient preparation.
The scrub nurse engages in brief periods of instrument table preparation separated by short idle gaps.
The scrub nurse assists the surgeon with a drill, prepares the instrument table, and holds surgical instruments with intermittent pauses.
The scrub nurse intermittently prepares the instrument table and holds surgical instruments with brief pauses between actions.
The scrub nurse briefly assists the head surgeon and manipulates the robotic arm before a short pause.
The scrub nurse prepares the instrument table in two separate bursts separated by a brief pause, followed by a long period of inactivity.
The scrub nurse assists the head surgeon by holding a hammer and then returns to preparing the instrument table.
The scrub nurse engages in a continuous sequence of preparing the instrument table and holding surgical instruments with brief pauses.
The scrub nurse assists the assistant surgeon and prepares the patient, interspersed with short idle periods.
The scrub nurse focuses on preparing the instrument table and holding surgical instruments with brief pauses in between.

level2_segments.summary: The scrub nurse performs initial setup and extensive preparation of the instrument table before the main procedure begins.
The nurse actively manipulates and calibrates the Mako robotic arm while managing surgical instruments.
The scrub nurse alternates between preparing the instrument table, assisting the surgical team, and cleaning the patient.
The nurse supports the surgeon with specific tools like a drill while maintaining instrument readiness.
The scrub nurse provides intermittent assistance with the robotic arm and specific instruments like a hammer while managing the table.
The nurse concludes the procedure by assisting the assistant surgeon, preparing the patient, and organizing the instrument table.

level1_segments.summary: The robotic system undergoes a comprehensive preoperative calibration sequence including arm status, homing, accuracy, brake, and angle discrepancy checks.
The surgeon performs initial surgery planning and conducts extended preoperative registration checks with intermittent patient timeouts.
The system executes the bone registration process to map the patient's anatomy.
Intraoperative planning is performed to define the surgical cuts and implant positioning.
The surgeon performs iterative bone preparation on various surfaces (anterior, posterior, distal) interspersed with frequent registration checks to verify alignment.
The robot monitor displayed bone preparation steps for both anterior and posterior surfaces with a brief pause in between.
The robot monitor showed the intra-operative planning phase.
The robot monitor indicated the completion of the procedure.

level2_segments.summary: The robotic system undergoes comprehensive preoperative calibration and the surgeon performs initial surgery planning with registration checks.
The system executes bone registration to map anatomy followed by intraoperative planning to define surgical cuts and implant positioning.
The surgeon performs iterative bone preparation on multiple surfaces with alignment verification, followed by a review of the preparation steps and planning phase.
The robot monitor indicates the completion of the surgical procedure.

robot setup (physical): Calibrate the robot by nurse

robot setup (physical): Install actual Saw tip

level1_segments.summary: The robot is powered on and undergoes initial calibration by the MPS.
The robot is dressed to ensure a sterile environment.
The nurse installs the saw and base array, performs calibration with the calibration array, and then removes the array.
The actual saw tip is installed onto the robot.

level2_segments.summary: The robot is powered on, calibrated, and dressed to establish a sterile operating environment.
The surgical saw and base array are installed and calibrated, followed by the attachment of the actual saw tip.

bone saw: head surgeon is touching; scrub nurse is touching

level1_segments.summary: The bone saw is held by the scrub nurse during an initial idle period.
The head surgeon takes possession of the bone saw and proceeds to use it for sawing.

level2_segments.summary: The bone saw transitions from an idle state held by the scrub nurse to active use by the head surgeon for bone cutting.

level1_segments.summary: The infrared tracker undergoes a series of brief manipulations by the robot technician interspersed with long idle periods, likely representing initial setup, calibration checks, and standby phases before the main procedure.
The robot technician engages in a prolonged, continuous manipulation of the infrared tracker, indicating its active use during the main surgical procedure.

level2_segments.summary: The infrared tracker transitions from initial setup and calibration checks to active continuous use during the main surgical procedure.
